title: Xerid Russian Beef Broil
description: Savory, springy and adventurous Russian dish featuring gelatine leaves and broil, perfect for a no-onion-no-garlic-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: gelatine leaves
ingredients.notes: soaked in cold water for at least 30 minutes

instructions: Preheat the broiler to its highest temperature setting.
Pat dry the beef strips with paper towels.
Season the beef with salt, black pepper, and a pinch of salt.
Dredge the beef strips in flour, shaking off any excess.
Melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the gelatine leaves to the saucepan and cook for 2-3 minutes or until dissolved.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the beef broth, beef fat, and milk.
Add the chopped dill to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Place the beef strips on a broiler pan lined with parchment paper.
Brush the beef strips with the gelatine mixture, making sure they're fully coated.
Broil the beef for 4-6 minutes per side or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Transfer the beef to a serving platter and garnish with chopped parsley.
Note: Traditionally, this dish is served with boiled potatoes or buckwheat groats, but feel free to serve it with your favorite sides!
